name: Structured World Model System Architecture
description: TypeScript/Node.js system for extracting structured world models from any input using LLM agents, with pipeline orchestration, validation, querying, and export capabilities

entities.name: Chunker Agent
entities.description: Splits large text inputs into manageable chunks that fit within LLM context limits, preserving context at boundaries

entities.name: Extraction Agent
entities.description: LLM-powered agent that extracts structured world models from raw input using specialized prompts for different source types

entities.name: Structuring Agent
entities.description: Converts raw extraction data into structured WorldModel format with proper ID mapping and validation

entities.name: Validation Agent
entities.description: Validates world model integrity by checking entity references, detecting orphans, and identifying issues

entities.name: Refinement Agent
entities.description: Incrementally updates existing world models with new input, extracting only deltas and changes

entities.name: Second Pass Agent
entities.description: Performs completeness analysis to find implicit entities, relations, and constraints missed in first pass

entities.name: Query Agent
entities.description: Answers natural language questions about world models using deterministic graph operations or LLM inference

entities.name: Pipeline
entities.description: Sequential stage execution framework with timing and progress callbacks for building world models

entities.name: World Model
entities.description: Structured representation containing entities, relations, processes, and constraints extracted from input

entities.name: Entity
entities.description: Something that exists in the world - actors, objects, systems, concepts, locations, events, groups, or resources

entities.name: Relation
entities.description: Directed connection between two entities with semantic type and human-readable label

entities.name: Process
entities.description: Dynamic sequence of steps that happen over time with actors, inputs, and outputs

entities.name: Constraint
entities.description: Invariant rule that must always hold true, with scope and severity level

entities.name: LLM Client
entities.description: Anthropic Claude integration with retry logic, timeout handling, and JSON parsing capabilities

entities.name: Graph Utilities
entities.description: Graph traversal and analysis functions for finding paths, dependencies, and generating visualizations

entities.name: CLI Tool
entities.description: Command-line interface providing model building, refinement, querying, and export functionality

entities.name: Schema Validation
entities.description: Zod-based schema validation for raw extractions with coercion and error recovery

entities.name: Export Formats
entities.description: Multiple export formats including Claude.md, system prompts, MCP schemas, Mermaid, and DOT

entities.name: Merge Engine
entities.description: Merges multiple world models with entity deduplication, ID remapping, and conflict resolution

entities.name: Timeline System
entities.description: Tracks world model evolution over time with snapshots, diffs, and entity history

entities.name: Algebra Operations
entities.description: Set-theoretic operations on world models: intersection, difference, and overlay

entities.name: Prompt Templates
entities.description: Source-type-specific prompts for code, conversation, document, URL, and mixed content analysis

entities.name: Token Estimator
entities.description: Estimates token count from text input using 4-character-per-token approximation

entities.name: ID Generator
entities.description: Generates unique identifiers with prefixes using cryptographic random bytes

relations.label: splits large inputs into processable chunks
selects appropriate prompt based on source type
makes agent calls for structured extraction
converts raw extraction into structured model
generates unique IDs for all model elements
merges existing model with extracted delta
analyzes model for missing implicit elements
falls back to inference for complex queries
estimates input size for chunking decisions
estimates tokens to determine chunk boundaries
uses diff functionality for snapshot comparison

processes.name: World Model Extraction
processes.description: Complete pipeline for extracting structured world models from raw input
processes.trigger: Raw input provided to buildWorldModel function

steps.action: Check input size and chunk if necessary
Extract entities, relations, processes, constraints using LLM
Convert raw data to structured world model format
Validate model integrity and check references

outcomes: Validated world model with confidence score and extraction notes

processes.name: Multi-Pass Extraction
processes.description: Enhanced extraction with multiple passes to find implicit elements

steps.action: Analyze model for missing implicit elements
Structure delta into world model format

outcomes: Enhanced world model with implicit elements discovered

processes.name: Model Refinement
processes.description: Incremental update of existing world model with new input
processes.trigger: refineWorldModel called with existing model and new input

steps.action: Summarize existing model for LLM context
Extract only new/changed elements from input

outcomes: Updated world model incorporating new input

processes.name: World Model Query
processes.description: Answer natural language questions about world models
processes.trigger: queryWorldModel called with model and question

steps.action: Fall back to LLM inference if no pattern matches

outcomes: Natural language answer with confidence and method used

processes.name: Timeline Snapshot
processes.description: Add world model snapshot to timeline with automatic diffing
processes.trigger: addSnapshot called or CLI snapshot command

steps.action: Compare model with previous snapshot if exists
Create snapshot with metadata and diff

outcomes: Timeline with new snapshot showing evolution

constraints.name: Entity ID Consistency
constraints.description: All relation source/target IDs must reference existing entity IDs

constraints.name: Process Participant Validity
constraints.description: All process participants and step actors must reference existing entity IDs

constraints.name: Constraint Scope Validity
constraints.description: All constraint scope entity IDs must reference existing entities

constraints.name: Chunk Size Limit
constraints.description: Text chunks must not exceed MAX_CHUNK_TOKENS (80,000) to fit in LLM context

constraints.name: Entity Name Uniqueness
constraints.description: Entity names should be unique within a world model to avoid confusion

constraints.name: JSON Output Format
constraints.description: LLM agents must output valid JSON matching expected schema

constraints.name: Relation Cycle Prevention
constraints.description: Self-referencing relations should be flagged as warnings

constraints.name: Process Step Ordering
constraints.description: Process steps must have sequential order numbers

constraints.name: CLI Command Authorization
constraints.description: CLI operations require appropriate file permissions for read/write

metadata.source_summary: Complete codebase analysis of SWM system including agents, pipeline, schema, utilities, CLI, and export functionality across 25+ TypeScript modules
extraction_notes: System follows clean agent-based architecture with clear separation of concerns
Heavy use of TypeScript for type safety and schema validation with Zod
LLM integration is well-abstracted with retry logic and error handling
CLI provides comprehensive functionality for all operations
Export formats enable integration with various AI systems and tools
Timeline and versioning capabilities support model evolution tracking
Algebra operations provide advanced model manipulation capabilities
Graph utilities enable rich querying and visualization features
